name: nuclear_norm_regularization
why: Penalize sum of singular values (nuclear norm) of weight matrices. From rate-distortion theory: concentrating energy in fewer basis coefficients improves lossy compression. Low-rank weights have fewer significant singular values, making quantization less destructive. Distinct from WD (penalizes magnitude/Frobenius, not rank) and QAT (quantization-specific noise).
validates: Nuclear norm regularization improves post-quant val_bpb by shaping weight structure for compressibility.
falsifies: Weight structure is already fine; quantization loss comes from outlier VALUES, not rank. Kill rank-based regularization.
notes: SVD is expensive — computed only on first micro-step. If ms/step increase is >10%, the throughput cost may kill it.
Watch both pre-quant AND post-quant. Pre-quant may be worse (constrained weights) but post-quant should be better.
If it wins, explore cheaper proxies (e.g., trace norm, randomized SVD).
